明白。杂务窗口已就位：查命令、改配置、简单问答；不写核心文件、不做决策、回答简短。

要处理什么？

---

_**User**_

这个功能在 Cursor 的 UI 界面里设置，不是 settings.json。操作路径如下：

开启方法（2步）：

打开 Cursor 设置：Ctrl + Shift + J → 选 Features 标签
找到 Agent 部分 → 开启 Auto-run mode（原名 YOLO mode）
或者：

在 Agent 聊天窗口底部，点击那个小齿轮/设置图标
找到 Auto-run 开关，打开即可
开启后，Agent 执行工具调用（读文件、写文件、运行命令）时不再弹出 View/Allow 确认框，全自动执行。

注意：Auto-run 对终端命令也会自动放行，建议同时设置一个命令黑名单（比如禁止 rm -rf、git push --force），在同一个设置页面里可以配置。

---

2. Or: Agent chat window bottom → gear/settings icon → Auto-run switch

They also note: Auto-run lets agent run tools without View/Allow prompts, but terminal commands get auto-approved too, so they suggest setting a command blacklist (e.g. rm -rf, git push --force) in the same settings page.

As the 杂务窗口, I should:
- Not write to core files
- Quick response, no lengthy expansion
- Acknowledge and maybe offer to save this somewhere if they want (but they didn't ask - they're just informing me)

I'll acknowledge briefly and note the blacklist tip. I won't write to 交接备忘 or core files unless they ask.
</details></think>

- **建议**：同页配命令黑名单，例如禁 `rm -rf`、`git push --force`。

[Cursor Docs](https://docs.cursor.com/agent/modes)<br/><br/># Cursor IDE Auto-Run Mode Agent Settings (2024-2025)<br/><br/>## What is Auto-Run Mode?<br/><br/>Auto-run mode (formerly called "YOLO Mode") is a setting in Cursor that allows the Agent to execute terminal commands automatically without requiring manual confirmation for each command.[4] This significantly improves productivity by eliminating repetitive approval clicks.<br/><br/>## How to Enable Auto-Run<br/><br/>1. Open Cursor Settings: `Cmd+Shift+J` (macOS) or `Ctrl+Shift+J` (Windows/Linux)<br/>2. Navigate to the Agent section<br/>3. Configure your auto-run prompt with allowed commands<br/>4. Toggle Auto-run to enabled[4]<br/><br/>## Configuring the Allow List<br/><br/>Auto-run uses natural language (not JSON) to describe safe commands. Recommended safe commands to allow:[4]<br/><br/>- **Tests:** vitest, jest, npm test, pytest, cargo test, go test<br/>- **Build commands:** npm run build, tsc, vite build, cargo build<br/>- **File operations:** mkdir, touch, cp, mv (but not rm -rf)<br/>- **Git read commands:** git status, git diff, git log, git branch<br/>- **Linting:** eslint, prettier, biome, ruff, clippy<br/>- **Package installs:** npm install, yarn add, pip install<br/><br/>## Commands to Always Require Approval<br/><br/>Deny these without confirmation:[4]<br/>- Production database or deployment commands<br/>- `docker rm`, `npm publish`<br/>- `git reset --hard`, `git push`<br/>- `rm -rf` and other destructive operations<br/><br/>## Current Implementation (v0.49.5)<br/><br/>As of version 0.49.5, when auto-run is enabled with an empty allowlist, commands still require manual confirmation (⌘+Enter).[1] This behavior is intentional for safety reasons rather than allowing all commands to execute automatically.[1] |

</details>

  "markdown": "# Plan Mode\n\nPlan Mode creates detailed implementation plans before writing any code. Agent researches your codebase, asks clarifying questions, and generates a reviewable plan you can edit before building.\n\nPress Shift+Tab from the chat input to rotate to Plan Mode. Cursor also suggests it automatically when you type keywords that indicate complex tasks.\n\n## How it works\n\n1. Click to build the plan when ready\n2. You review and edit the plan through chat or markdown files\n3. Creates a comprehensive implementation plan\n4. Researches your codebase to gather relevant context\n5. Agent asks clarifying questions to understand your requirements\n\nPlans are saved by default in your home directory. Click \"Save to workspace\" to move it to your workspace for future reference, team sharing, and documentation.\n\n## When to use Plan Mode\n\nPlan Mode works best for:\n\n- Architectural decisions where you want to review the approach first\n- Unclear requirements where you need to explore before understanding scope\n- Tasks that touch many files or systems\n- Complex features with multiple valid approaches\n\nFor quick changes or tasks you've done many times before, jumping straight to Agent mode is fine.\n\n## Starting over from a plan\n\nSometimes Agent builds something that doesn't match what you wanted. Instead of trying to fix it through follow-up prompts, go back to the plan.\n\nRevert the changes, refine the plan to be more specific about what you need, and run it again. This is often faster than fixing an in-progress agent, and produces cleaner results.\n\nFor larger changes, spend extra time creating a precise, well-scoped plan. The hard part is often figuring out what change should be made. With the right instructions, delegate implementation to Agent.\n\n## Switching modes\n\n- Press Shift+Tab for quick switching\n- Use the mode picker dropdown in Agent\n\n---\n\n## Sitemap\n\n[Overview of all docs pages](https://cursor.com/llms.txt)"
}

{
  "url": "https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/security",
  "markdown": "# Security &amp; Network\n\nCloud Agents are available in Privacy Mode. We never train on your code and only retain code for running the agent. [Learn more about Privacy mode](https://www.cursor.com/privacy-overview).\n\n## Secret protection\n\nSecrets provided to Cloud Agents are encrypted at rest and in transit. They are not visible to anyone other than the Cloud Agent user.\n\nYou can classify secrets as \"Redacted\" for additional protection. Redacted secrets:\n\n- Are redacted from model tool calls, so they are not shown to the models or stored in chat transcripts\n- Are scanned in commit messages and files, which are rejected if they contain the secret\n\nThis prevents accidental exposure of credentials in version control and model context.\n\n## Signed commits\n\nCloud Agents sign every commit with a HSM-backed GPG key. On GitHub and GitLab, these commits display a \"Verified\" badge so your team can confirm the commit came from Cursor.\n\nThis works automatically for all Cloud Agents. No setup is required.\n\nIf your repository enforces branch protection rules that require signed commits, Cloud Agent PRs satisfy those rules without extra configuration.\n\n## What you should know\n\n1. If you disable privacy mode when starting a cloud agent, then enable it during the agent's run, the agent continues with privacy mode disabled until it completes.\n2. If privacy mode is disabled, we collect prompts and dev environments to improve the product.\n3. The agent auto-runs all terminal commands, letting it iterate on tests. This differs from the foreground agent, which requires user approval for every command. Auto-running introduces data exfiltration risk: attackers could execute prompt injection attacks, tricking the agent to upload code to malicious websites. See [OpenAI's explanation about risks of prompt injection for cloud agents](https://platform.openai.com/docs/codex/agent-network#risks-of-agent-internet-access).\n4. The agent has internet access by default. You can configure [network egress controls](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/security-network.md#network-access) to restrict the domains the agent can access.\n5. Your code runs inside our AWS infrastructure in isolated VMs and is stored on VM disks while the agent is accessible.\n6. Grant read-write privileges to our GitHub app for repos you want to edit. We use this to clone the repo and make changes.\n\n## Network access\n\nControl which network resources your Cloud Agents can reach. These settings are available on the [Cloud Agents dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=cloud-agents) for individual users and team admins.\n\n### Access modes\n\nThree modes control outbound network access for Cloud Agents:\n\n| Mode | Behavior |\n| --- | --- |\n| Allow all network access | Cloud Agents can reach any external host. No domain restrictions apply. |\n| Default + allowlist | Cloud Agents can reach the [default domains](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/terminal.md#default-network-allowlist) plus any domains you add to your allowlist. |\n| Allowlist only | Cloud Agents can only reach the domains you explicitly add to your allowlist. |\n\nEven in Allowlist only mode, a small set of domains remain accessible so Cloud Agents can function. These include Cursor's own services and source control management (SCM) providers.\n\n### User-level settings\n\nIndividual users can configure their network access mode from the [Cloud Agents dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=cloud-agents) under the Security header. Your user-level setting applies to all Cloud Agents you create.\n\nWhen you select a mode that includes an allowlist (Default + allowlist or Allowlist only), an allowlist configuration section appears below the setting where you can add your custom domains.\n\n### Team-level settings\n\nTeam admins can set a default network access mode for the entire team from the same dashboard. The team-level allowlist is the same allowlist that admins configure for the [sandbox default network allowlist](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/terminal.md#default-network-allowlist). There is no separate allowlist to manage; one allowlist controls both Cloud Agent network access and the sandbox defaults.\n\nWhen a team-level setting exists:\n\n- If a user has not configured a setting, the team default applies.\n- If a user has configured their own setting, the user setting takes precedence.\n\n### Locking the setting (Enterprise)\n\nLocking is available for Enterprise teams only.\n\nEnterprise team admins can lock the network access setting using the Lock Network Access Policy option. When locked:\n\n- Users cannot override the locked setting from their own dashboard.\n- The team-level setting applies to every member, regardless of their individual preference.\n\nThis gives admins full control over Cloud Agent network access across the organization.\n\n### Relationship to sandbox network policy\n\nThe \"Default\" domains in the Default + allowlist mode are the same [default network allowlist](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/terminal.md#default-network-allowlist) used by the desktop Agent's sandbox. The team-level allowlist is also shared: when an admin configures an allowlist on the dashboard, it applies to both Cloud Agent network access and the [sandbox network policy](https://cursor.com/docs/reference/sandbox.md).\n\n## Egress IP ranges\n\nCloud Agents make network connections from specific IP address ranges when accessing external services, APIs, or repositories.\n\n### API endpoint\n\nThe IP ranges are available via a [JSON API endpoint](https://cursor.com/docs/ips.json):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncurl https://cursor.com/docs/ips.json\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Response format\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"version\": 1,\n  \"modified\": \"2025-09-24T16:00:00.000Z\",\n  \"cloudAgents\": {\n    \"us3p\": [\"100.26.13.169/32\", \"34.195.201.10/32\", \"...\"],\n    \"us4p\": [\"54.184.235.255/32\", \"35.167.37.158/32\", \"...\"],\n    \"us5p\": [\"3.12.82.200/32\", \"52.14.104.140/32\", \"...\"]\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- cloudAgents: Object containing IP ranges, keyed by cluster\n- modified: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the IP ranges were last updated\n- version: Schema version number for the API response\n\nIP ranges published in [CIDR notation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing). You can use an online conversion tool to convert from CIDR notation to IP address ranges if needed.\n\n### Using the IP ranges\n\nThese published IP ranges may be used by Cloud Agents to:\n\n- Access web resources during agent execution\n- Make API calls to external services\n- Download packages and dependencies\n- Clone and push to remote repositories (unless using the [GitHub IP allow list](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/github.md#ip-allow-list-configuration))\n\nIf your organization uses firewall rules or IP allowlists to control network access, you may need to allowlist these IP ranges to ensure Cloud Agents can properly access your services.\n\nImportant considerations:\n\n- If you must use these IP ranges, we strongly encourage regular monitoring of the JSON API endpoint.\n- We do not recommend allowlisting by IP address as your primary security mechanism.\n- We make changes to our IP addresses from time to time for scaling and operational needs.\n\n### GitHub proxy and IP allow list\n\nCursor supports a similar but distinct feature to [use a GitHub egress proxy for IP allow lists](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/github.md#ip-allow-list-configuration). This proxy works for all GitHub-dependent Cursor features, including Cloud Agents.\n\nWe recommended that you use the GitHub-specific IP allow list for GitHub, as it is more deeply integrated with the Cursor GitHub app, and the above egress IP ranges for everything else.\n\n---\n\n## Sitemap\n\n[Overview of all docs pages](https://cursor.com/llms.txt)"
}

可以按下面两种方式找，不同版本入口可能不一样：

**方式 1：设置里搜**
- `Ctrl + Shift + J` 打开 Cursor Settings  
- 在设置顶部的**搜索框**里输入 **`auto run`** 或 **`auto-run`**  
- 看结果里有没有 “Auto-run” / “自动运行” 的开关

**方式 2：聊天窗口里**
- 打开 **Agent 聊天**（不是普通 Chat）  
- 看输入框**左下角**或**底部**有没有「自动运行」或齿轮图标  
- 点进去找 Auto-run 开关

若方式 1 搜不到，可能是你当前版本把这项挪到了 Agent 面板里，优先用方式 2。

</details>

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

User saved the setting (Ask Every Time) but still no confirmation popup when I make edits. They're asking if it's related to having previously authorized the agent to "run everything" - maybe there's a session-level or per-chat authorization that persists.

Possible reasons:
1. Cursor might have "remembered" the previous Auto-run authorization for this session/workspace
2. There could be a separate "approve all" or session-level setting
3. The setting might need Cursor restart to take effect
4. There might be a per-conversation "allow all" that overrides the global setting

I should give a short 杂务-style answer: 有可能。之前选过「运行所有」的话，当前会话或工作区可能被记成「已授权」，所以仍不弹窗。建议：关掉这个 Agent 聊天窗口、新开一个再试；或重启 Cursor 后再试一次。
</details></think>

---


有可能有关。

如果之前在这台机器/这个项目里选过「运行所有」或对某次对话点过「全部允许」，Cursor 有时会记住这次授权，当前会话里就继续不弹窗。

可以试这两步：

1. **关掉当前这个 Agent 聊天**，新开一个 Agent 对话，再让我做一次小修改，看会不会弹确认。
2. 若仍不弹，**完全退出 Cursor 再重新打开**，然后再试一次修改。

若这样仍不弹，就属于 Cursor 的授权/会话逻辑，杂务这边无法改，需要把「已改为 Ask Every Time 仍不弹窗」汇报给大脑，由工程或架构师查文档/反馈。

_**User**_

自动弹窗怎么设置

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

User is asking how to set up "自动弹窗" - the confirmation popup when Agent runs tools (the diff/approval dialog). They want the IDE to show the prompt every time instead of auto-running. So they're asking for the setting to get the "Ask Every Time" / approval popup behavior.

I already told them to use "Ask Every Time" in Cursor Settings → Agents → Auto-Run Mode, but they said it didn't work (maybe session had prior approval). So now they're asking how to set the "自动弹窗" - I'll give the exact path again and add that this is the only in-UI way; if it still doesn't pop, it may be a Cursor bug/session state.
</details></think>

---


**在 Cursor 里让「每次执行都弹窗」：**

1. `Ctrl + Shift + J` 打开 Cursor Settings  
2. 左侧点 **Agents**  
3. 在 **Auto-Run** 里把 **Auto-Run Mode** 选成 **Ask Every Time**

这样工具执行（改文件、跑命令等）都会先弹确认，不会自动跑。

如果改完仍不弹窗：关掉当前 Agent 对话、新开一个再试，或重启 Cursor。仍不行就可能是当前会话/工作区被记成「已授权」，需反馈给 Cursor 或大脑排查。

---
